title: Model Trained Exclusively on Synthetic Low-Light Data
description: ResNet-50 trained on COCO images processed through the statistical low-light synthesis pipeline, then evaluated on multi-domain benchmarks

primary_validation.task: video action recognition in extremely dark conditions
primary_validation.ellar_note: ELL = Extreme Low-Light conditions (ground-truth evaluation on Ellar's extremely dark videos)
primary_validation.lowlight_note: LL = Low-light subset of Ellar dataset

key_findings.claim: Synthetic low-light data matches extreme degradation patterns better than moderate low-light datasets
key_findings.validation_approach: Train only on synthetic data → test on real extreme degradation (Ellar)
key_findings.result: Model generalizes well despite extreme degradation, suggests synthetic distribution accurately captures real low-light physics
key_findings.statistical_distribution_quality: Right-skewed log-normal noise, beta-weighted contrast/saturation sampling creates realistic diversity
key_findings.illumination_darkening_effectiveness: Bilateral filter decomposition preserves edge detail while darkening shadows (prevents artificial flattening)

noise_model_validation.validation: Models trained on synthetic data transfer to real low-light without overfitting noise artifacts

limitations_and_notes: Pre-trained on ImageNet which provides strong backbone features
Evaluation on Ellar shows generalization to extreme conditions
LL subset results (80.32% → 99.44% top1/5) show excellent performance on standard low-light
ELL subset results (18.58%) reflect extreme difficulty of Ellar dataset itself (designed to be notoriously hard)
Current implementation uses TensorFlow 1.x (TF 2.x migration planned for future releases)
data_source: Validation logs from Ellar dataset evaluation
